explanation: This command simulates a benign use of 'whoami' but could be used to test if the Sigma rule detects 'whoami' in the command line. It uses a standard Windows command-line interface.
This command uses PowerShell to execute 'whoami'. It is a valid command line that includes 'whoami' and can be used to test the Sigma rule in a controlled environment.
This command directly executes the 'whoami.exe' binary. It is a valid command line that includes 'whoami' and can be used to test the Sigma rule in a controlled environment.
